STG.indicator: Have a plan to achieve own team's goals

scale.5: Consistently develops and communicates clear, actionable plans aligned with team and organizational goals, ensuring team focus and accountability.
scale.3: Creates basic plans to achieve team goals but may lack clarity or follow-through.
scale.1: Does not create plans or goals for the team, leading to confusion or misalignment.

STG.indicator: Demonstrate an understanding of the organization's mission and strategies

scale.5: Clearly articulates how team objectives align with the organization’s mission and incorporates strategic priorities in decisions.
scale.3: Understands the mission and strategies but inconsistently applies them in daily work.
scale.1: Lacks awareness of or disregards the organization’s mission and strategies.

STG.indicator: Understand the overall picture of the situation (general themes, trends, goals)

scale.5: Anticipates changes and identifies patterns or trends that guide proactive decision-making.
scale.3: Recognizes general themes or trends but rarely uses them to inform decisions.
scale.1: Focuses only on immediate issues without grasping broader context.

INN.indicator: Encourage others to look at problems and processes in new ways

scale.5: Inspires and facilitates creative thinking among team members, regularly leading to innovative improvements.
scale.3: Occasionally promotes alternative thinking but without consistent follow-up.
scale.1: Discourages or ignores novel approaches and sticks rigidly to existing methods.

INN.indicator: Find ways to increase productivity or streamline processes

scale.5: Proactively identifies and implements efficient processes, significantly improving team output.
scale.3: Suggests or adopts minor improvements in productivity or workflow.
scale.1: Fails to recognize inefficiencies or resists changes to established processes.

INN.indicator: Challenge "the way it has always been done"

scale.5: Proactively questions outdated practices, proposes innovative alternatives, and leads change initiatives with measurable benefits.
scale.3: Occasionally challenges existing methods and suggests alternatives but may not always follow through.
scale.1: Accepts current practices without question and resists considering new approaches.

CSF.indicator: Solve customer problems, involving others when needed

scale.5: Quickly resolves complex customer issues by leveraging team expertise and cross-functional collaboration.
scale.3: Handles standard problems and seeks help for more complex issues.
scale.1: Struggles to resolve customer problems or fails to seek help when necessary.

CSF.indicator: Make sure customers are satisfied with the solution provided

scale.5: Follows up and ensures complete customer satisfaction with proactive communication and resolution.
scale.3: Delivers appropriate solutions but limited in follow-up.
scale.1: Does not check if the customer is satisfied or fails to address concerns fully.

CSF.indicator: Ask for feedback from customers to identify opportunities for improvement

scale.5: Proactively seeks detailed feedback from customers and uses it to implement effective changes.
scale.3: Occasionally asks for feedback and considers it for improvements.
scale.1: Rarely or never requests feedback from customers; ignores input.

OPX.indicator: Convey expectations for assignments clearly and precisely

scale.5: Consistently provides clear, detailed instructions and expectations, reducing misunderstandings.
scale.3: Communicates expectations with general clarity but may miss important details.
scale.1: Gives vague or incomplete directions, causing confusion.

OPX.indicator: Routinely check work outcomes to ensure quality

scale.5: Implements regular quality checks and follows up to maintain high standards.
scale.3: Checks work occasionally and addresses issues when noticed.
scale.1: Neglects to review work outcomes; problems often go unnoticed.

OPX.indicator: Remove obstacles in order to move the work forward and/or get efforts back on track

scale.5: Quickly identifies and removes roadblocks, ensuring timely progress.
scale.3: Addresses issues when raised but may not anticipate obstacles.
scale.1: Fails to act on or recognize blockers, delaying progress.

INS.indicator: Shadow younger team memebers to support their professional growth

scale.5: Regularly mentors juniors, providing guidance and learning opportunities.
scale.3: Offers occasional support or guidance to younger team members.
scale.1: Shows little interest in mentoring or supporting junior staff.

INS.indicator: Openly and proactively share own knowledge and expertise

scale.5: Freely shares knowledge, encourages learning, and is seen as a go-to expert.
scale.3: Shares knowledge when asked but not consistently proactive.
scale.1: Withholds information or avoids sharing expertise.

INS.indicator: Help employees develop awareness of their strengths and weaknesses

scale.5: Regularly coaches others in a constructive way to build self-awareness and growth.
scale.3: Provides occasional feedback to help others reflect on their performance.
scale.1: Avoids giving feedback or offers only vague praise or criticism.

INF.indicator: Provide comprehensive and convincing rational arguments to support own ideas and proposals

scale.5: Consistently presents well-structured arguments that win support and drive decisions.
scale.3: Presents logical arguments but may lack depth or clarity.
scale.1: Struggles to justify proposals or ideas clearly.

INF.indicator: Express own positions taking into account the interests and concerns of the interlocutors

scale.5: Balances own views with empathy and adapts approach based on audience needs.
scale.3: Generally considers others’ views but may lack flexibility in discussion.
scale.1: Disregards others’ concerns and insists solely on own viewpoint.

INF.indicator: Actively solicit support for ideas and proposals from others

scale.5: Engages others early in the idea process, building broad support and collaboration.
scale.3: Seeks input when necessary but may not actively involve others.
scale.1: Pursues ideas alone without consulting or involving stakeholders.

RES.indicator: Work productively in the face of ambiguity or uncertainty

scale.5: Thrives in unclear environments, making sound decisions and maintaining output.
scale.3: Maintains productivity in ambiguity but may seek frequent reassurance.
scale.1: Struggles to act without clear guidance; becomes unproductive.

RES.indicator: Maintain clariry of mind and optimism even in the most unfavorable circumstances

scale.5: Remains calm, focused, and inspires confidence in others under pressure.
scale.3: Stays composed but may show signs of stress in difficult times.
scale.1: Panics or becomes negative, affecting team morale.

RES.indicator: Constructively handle mistakes, accepting responsibility and learning from experience

scale.5: Openly acknowledges errors, learns quickly, and shares lessons with others.
scale.3: Admits to mistakes and adjusts behavior when prompted.
scale.1: Avoids accountability or repeats the same mistakes.

LRN.indicator: Ask for feedback to improve own performance

scale.5: Regularly seeks out feedback and implements changes to improve performance.
scale.3: Open to feedback when offered, but rarely initiates requests.
scale.1: Avoids or dismisses feedback; shows little interest in self-improvement.

LRN.indicator: Show interest and energy regarding their growth in the organization

scale.5: Actively seeks growth opportunities and demonstrates commitment to career progression.
scale.3: Shows general interest in growth but takes limited initiative.
scale.1: Displays little enthusiasm or effort toward personal development.

LRN.indicator: Look for additional responsibilities that allow them to develop new skills

scale.5: Frequently takes on new challenges and uses them to build relevant skills.
scale.3: Accepts new tasks when assigned, with occasional interest in learning.
scale.1: Avoids taking on additional responsibilities or resists skill development.
